title: Logs, metrics, and traces answer different questions
description: Metrics tell you something is wrong and how much, traces show where in the request path, and logs explain why — using one for another's job is costly or blind.
body: Use when choosing which signal to add or query. Metrics are cheap aggregates good for alerting and trends but lack per-request detail; traces reconstruct a single request's path across services to localize latency/errors; logs carry rich per-event context for root cause. Do not page on logs, search traces for aggregate trends, or store high-cardinality detail in metrics. Actions: alert on metrics, localize with traces, confirm cause with correlated logs sharing a trace/request id. Validation signals include a trace id linking a metric anomaly to the explanatory log line. Risk boundary: relying on a single pillar leaves blind spots; flag missing correlation ids that prevent pivoting between them.
